persona.name: The filmmaker-patron
persona.description: Viewers who discover indie short filmmakers online and want to financially support them directly — one film at a time — where their money reaches the maker, not a subscription pool.
persona.frustration: All-you-can-watch platforms dilute creator payouts to pennies. Tip jars decouple support from the actual film. There is no simple way to buy a single short film and know the payment goes straight to the filmmaker.

problem: The patronage act is broken: you can subscribe to a platform, or donate to a tip jar, but you cannot buy the film and have that money go to the filmmaker. OneReel is the smallest possible fix.
positioning: The Patron's Channel — your money reaches the maker, not the platform.
hero_line: Be the reason the next one gets made.

catalog.domain: short films (indie, under 25 min, fictional catalog)

commerce_behavior.cart: single-item (one film per purchase, not a bundle)
commerce_behavior.checkout: guest checkout — no account required for first purchase
commerce_behavior.fulfillment: digital — film unlocked immediately on payment confirmation

rights_provenance.note: Sandbox only — no real film rights. All catalog items are fictional.
rights_provenance.media: Placeholder assets only — no real footage committed to repo

architecture.data_layer: packages/commerce-core (local deterministic engine, no external DB or payment)
architecture.ui_source: docs/atm-415/ui/onereel — port verbatim, no visual changes

kpi_assumptions.nonclaim: Synthetic cohort only. Numbers do not predict or reflect real patron behavior.

risks.description: UI port may deviate from provided design
risks.mitigation: impeccable critique gate >= 7/10, zero P0 findings, before qa passes

risks.description: Synthetic patron behavior may differ from real film buyer behavior
risks.mitigation: Synthetic-only nonclaim recorded in every iteration verdict
Noted as a known simplification in the spec; not a defect

nonclaims: does not prove real payment processing
does not prove real film rights or distribution
does not prove real filmmaker payout
synthetic cohort only — not real traffic or patron behavior
no claim of live deployment or production traffic

stop_conditions.ENGINEERING_REQUIRED: If a required adapter, schema, or behavioral invariant is missing from commerce-core and blocks the build — stop and surface it
stop_conditions.OWNER_GATE: Any change to frozen contracts/seeds/KPI after baseline; any real payment, deployment, or user contact

bounded_adaptation_candidate.description: CTA framing change: 'Back this film' vs 'Buy film' — does patronage framing move conversion?
